title: melia azedarach var. sambucina
intro: Melia azedarach var. sambucina, also known as the Persian lilac or yellow melia, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to parts of Asia and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and cold symptoms, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
